summary: Frontend-only testing of (a) clickable locked badges with route navigation + progress bars, and (b) new #howtopray view. All 14 review-request features pass. 46/46 individual UI checks green across PL/EN and light/dark/romantic themes.
critical_code_review_comments: app.js is 4851 lines — well above the 700-line guideline. Splitting into modules (badges.js, prayer.js, koran.js, islam-views.js) is recommended for maintainability. Same recommendation as iteration_1.
Tailwind is loaded via cdn.tailwindcss.com (console warning: 'should not be used in production'). Consider switching to PostCSS/CLI build for production.

Minor: badges() seeds learnedLetters in the user's state; if user already has any learned letter, badge-first_letter is auto-marked unlocked, so the link becomes a non-clickable check. This is correct behavior but worth documenting for QA seeds — use learnedLetters:[] to keep first_letter locked.
test_credentials: n/a — no auth in this PWA
seed_data_creation: Used localStorage.alif-ai-state with badges:[], learnedLetters:[], points:0, streak:0 to keep all 16 badges locked. Also tested with badges:['first_letter'] to verify unlocked state hides data-testid and shows ✓.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All assertions from the review-request pass. Verified: 16 total badges, 15 locked when state has 1 learned letter (16 locked when learnedLetters=[]), each locked card has progress bar + → arrow + data-testid='badge-{id}', click on locked routes correctly to #home/#lessons/#koran/#games/#muallaf, unlocked badges have ✓ and no testid, hint text PL/EN both render. #howtopray view shows wrapper, rakah explanation, 5 prayer cards (Fajr=2, Dhuhr=4, Asr=4, Maghrib=3, Isha=4), 4 prereq cards (wudu/wear/qibla/niyyah), 11 step cards (takbir→salam), CTA → #prayer. Prayer header button → #howtopray. #islam first link is islam-link-howtopray → #howtopray. EN strings render. light/dark/romantic themes all render howtopray correctly.
